methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Thoron is a seed-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe consists of 8 private firms operating in this space, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Airship Industries. No public companies provide direct comparability.

Peer-median valuation was not applicable. The sector contains only 4 same-sector peers, and none satisfied the filtering criteria of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier simultaneously. The analyst required a minimum of 3 datapoints to support a median-based approach; applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable breadth bias given the heterogeneity of business models and capital requirements within Drones & Autonomous.

As a result, no triangulated valuation range can be supported by the available data. The analysis identifies a material constraint: the Drones & Autonomous sector at seed stage lacks sufficient peer density for conventional comparable-company valuation. Forward work should either (1) expand the observation window to adjacent stages if stage-specific peers remain sparse, (2) employ alternative methods such as venture-capital method or discounted cash flow if financial projections are available, or (3) reconsider peer selection criteria if current filters are overly restrictive. The key uncertainty remains the applicability of later-stage drone and autonomous vehicle peers to a seed-stage valuation.
